pi_items.source: Confirmed by analyzing setup_opentoonz.sh, which downloads official OpenToonz samples from GitHub and places them in /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/.
Confirmed by analyzing setup_task.sh and export_result.sh, which explicitly set and use OUTPUT_DIR='/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/drift'.
Confirmed by task instructions and export_result.sh, which searches for and accepts files matching '*20.png'.
Confirmed by task instructions and export_result.sh, which searches for and accepts files matching '*60.png'.
Contradicted by verifier.py. The verifier calculates an absolute pixel difference ratio (non_zero_count / total_pixels) and expects a value >= 0.01, rather than using SSIM as claimed.
Confirmed by verifier.py, which calculates x-axis centroids and awards points if cx60 > cx20 (rightward motion).

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the official OpenToonz sample scene 'dwanko_run.tnz', located at '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The agent must render frames to the directory '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/drift/'. The verifier evaluates success by checking for rightward motion, specifically calculating the horizontal centroid of the character and ensuring the x-coordinate in Frame 60 is strictly greater than in Frame 20. It also enforces a minimum absolute pixel difference ratio of 0.01 between the two frames, contradicting the metadata's claim of using an SSIM threshold of 0.05.
